overview: Grixis Tide Familiars is a Pauper combo deck that leverages the newly legal High Tide to generate massive mana for its win conditions. It utilizes Nightscape Familiar to discount spells and provide regeneration, while relying on Stream of Thought or infinite Murmuring Mystic tokens to close games. The deck also features a flexible Ardent Elementalist and Snapcaster Mage mini-combo alongside strong black removal.
win_conditions: Ardent Elementalist and Snapcaster Mage combo

keep_criteria: Cantrips to manipulate the top of the deck and create card advantage
Combo pieces like High Tide, Mulldrifter, and Nightscape Familiar
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (fetches count as 1.5), at least one cantrip (Opt, Ponder, Brainstorm), and a Familiar or Lorien Revealed.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with no cantrips or deck manipulation
Hands that cannot execute the game plan early
Mulligan any hand with three or more lands, no draw spells, or only one land with no early plays.

examples.reason: Has great land drops, cantrips for card advantage, and combo potential with High Tide.
Greedy but rewarding; can generate massive mana early and evoke Mulldrifter.
Cannot do anything until land drops are found; lacks cantrips or deck manipulation.

early_game: Play lands and cantrips like Preordain to find combo pieces
Cast Nightscape Familiar to discount red and blue spells
Set up High Tide for mana acceleration
Turn 1: Play a land, cast a cantrip to dig for Sunscape Familiar or Lorien Revealed.
If holding a fetch, use it to find a dual land that enters untapped to enable early spells.
Turn 2: Cast Familiar to start fueling the graveyard, or continue cantripping if you have no threats.
Prioritize setting up the engine over committing to the board unless facing aggressive decks that require early interaction.
mid_game: Execute the Ardent Elementalist and Snapcaster Mage mini-combo
Use Snuff Out to remove problematic creatures
Play Mulldrifter for card advantage and board presence
Treat your graveyard as a secondary hand.
Cast spells from it only when necessary to enable combos or stabilize.
Preserve cantrips to dig through disruption.
your value comes from card advantage, not board presence.
Commit threats only when you have a clear path to resolving Lorien Revealed or when you can trade up using Snap + Familiar.
late_game: Cast Stream of Thought for a massive draw and win
Activate Murmuring Mystic to generate infinite 1/1 Bird tokens
Regenerate Nightscape Familiar to block and attack repeatedly
Lorien Revealed is the primary win condition
resolve it and draw into game-ending lands or spells.
Snap + Sunscape Familiar can win instantly if you have a free spell in the graveyard.
Use cantrips to assemble these pieces while maintaining card advantage.
Go all-in when you have enough resources to resolve Lorien Revealed despite potential counters (e.g., you have Snap, multiple cantrips, or the opponent has tapped out).
Commit when you can trade a Familiar for a key removal spell and still win via card advantage.

key_cards.name: High Tide
key_cards.notes: Enables the combo by turning lands into blue mana.

key_cards.name: Stream of Thought
key_cards.notes: Primary way to beat opponents using High Tide mana.

key_cards.name: Murmuring Mystic
key_cards.notes: Alternative engine to create infinite 1/1 Bird tokens.

key_cards.name: Nightscape Familiar
key_cards.notes: Discounts red and blue spells, can regenerate and attack.

key_cards.name: Ardent Elementalist
key_cards.notes: Part of a mini-combo with Snapcaster Mage; costs less with multiple Familiars.

key_cards.name: Snapcaster Mage
key_cards.notes: Works with Ardent Elementalist to replay spells for free.

key_cards.name: Snuff Out
key_cards.notes: One of the best removal spells in the format, enabled by black mana.

key_cards.name: Preordain
key_cards.notes: Manipulates the top of the deck to find combo pieces.

key_cards.name: Mulldrifter
key_cards.notes: Provides card draw and can be evoked for tempo.

sequencing_tips: Discount counterspells like Negate using Nightscape Familiar for efficiency.
Look for windows in the opponent's strategy to safely play your combo.
Use Snuff Out early to clear the board before executing win conditions.
Prioritize land drops and cantrips in opening hands to avoid dead turns.
Familiars is highly vulnerable to graveyard hate
always have a plan B (like Snap + Familiar or direct damage) if Rest in Peace or Null Rod is in play.
Lorien Revealed is a combo piece, not a board wipe
use it to draw into wins, not to stabilize.
Avoid overusing cantrips early if you have no threats
preserve them for when you need to dig through disruption.

matchups.advice: Side in Snuff Out and Negate. Use Nightscape Familiar as a blocker and regenerate it. Wait for a window to play the combo.
Side in Cast into the Fire to exile indestructible lands and deal with problematic permanents.
Side in interaction to handle aggressive burn. Note that Grixis lacks God-Pharaoh's Faithful for life gain, making it vulnerable.
Side in removal and interaction. Use Familiars' removal to clear their creatures and develop your game plan.
Side in Pyroblast to counter Slippery Bogle on the stack. Treat the matchup as a race.

Use Snap to cast free spells instantly, bypassing counterspells. Dig deeper with cantrips to find Lorien Revealed or additional threats. If facing heavy counters, commit multiple spells in a row or use Snap to win before they can react.

Disrupt their mana with early interaction or targeted removal. Use cantrips to dig faster than they can ramp. If they establish a board, use Snap + Familiar to combo off or trade up with card advantage.

Prioritize early interaction (Spell Pierce, Lightning Axe) to stabilize. Use cantrips to dig for removal or Lorien Revealed. Race if you can stabilize and have enough threats to outpace their damage. Do not overcommit to the board; value out their creatures with efficient removal.

Out-value them with relentless cantrips and graveyard-fueled spells. Use Snap to bypass counterspells and win instantly. Dig for Lorien Revealed to draw into game-ending resources. Pressure their hand size and force them to spend resources on disruption.

Grind for card advantage with cantrips and Familiar. Track their graveyard to avoid falling for Snap + Familiar combos. Sideboard in additional graveyard hate and counterspells. Win by out-drawing them and resolving Lorien Revealed first.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells (especially on your second spell), targeted removal (Lightning Axe, Go for the Throat), and graveyard hate (Rest in Peace, Null Rod, Void Rend).
Note when opponents tap out for disruption
this is your window to dig for Lorien or Snap.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: The main game plan is to take advantage of the mana High Tide creates so you can beat your opponent with Stream of Thought, but this deck also plays Murmuring Mystic as an alternative win condition.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: beat your opponent with Stream of Thought
Murmuring Mystic as an alternative win condition. You can use it as an engine to create infinite 1/1 Bird tokens.
Ardent Elementalist is part of a mini-combo with Snap

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Negate is a powerful counter, particularly if you discount its cost with Nightscape.
Try to be smart and look for a window in your opponent's strategy to play your combo.
Snuff Out is excellent to deal with their board
Blue decks usually recover well from mulligans because of their cantrips

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Thraben Charm is a bit problematic
Pyroblast might seem a weird sideboard choice, but it is actually one of the few ways to stop Slippery Bogle
deal with Nihil Spellbomb and Blood Fountain, which are quite problematic
